---
title: "Primary vs. Secondary evaluation criteria"
author: "Experiments in Assessment WG"
date: 2026-04-09
date-modified: last-modified
categories: ["Level 0", "Aim: Value process", "Aim: Bias mitigation", "CoARA Commitment 6", "Target: Funder", "Target: Academic Institution", "Target: Research Group"]
description: "A secondary set of evaluation criteria that would be used in the event of proposals (or whatever is evaluated) that are of the same quality. Potential tiebreakers could be used to promote aspects of research culture, diversity, or other things that are important to the evaluating body."
image: false
---

::: {.callout-warning icon=false}

## Objectives and potential outcome

:::

## Research domains

## Context and considerations

This idea is linked to the **[EDI Tiebreak](../edi-tiebreak/index.qmd)** idea, but it could be expanded to include any types of elements chosen by the assessment committees. The elements for a secondary evaluation should be made explicit to candidates. 

## Challenges and mitigations

## Evaluating success

## Related CoARA commitments

# Relevant resources and literature 

## Templates from funders and institutions 

## Case examples and literature

## Other resources

# Case Studies or Implementation Examples
